subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Chemical Hazard Labels
description: A 60-second lesson on Chemical Hazard Labels.

hook.text: A skull and crossbones. A flame. An exclamation mark. These symbols could save your life.

buildup.text: The Globally Harmonized System uses standard pictograms to communicate chemical hazards.

discovery.text: Nine pictograms cover health hazards, physical hazards, and environmental hazards.

twist.text: Safety Data Sheets provide 16 sections of detail—but most workers never read past section 2.

climax.text: Knowing the pictograms gives you instant hazard awareness without reading the full document.

punchline.text: Nine symbols. Universal language. Learn them once, read danger everywhere.

quiz.question: How many pictograms does the Globally Harmonized System use?
